Hello, hello, and welcome to Seven Figure Emails installment number five. We actually have a change of pace today. We are going to do a three part post webinar replay sequence. So kind of a three in one for three different segments after a webinar. So segment one, we're going to look at the five, the fab five template segment two, the no goes shall pass and segment three, the traffic light. You'll see what all those segments are and what these templates are in just a second, but a little bit of background. So a lot of the launches I write for in a lot of the long and a lot of the ones we do at copy hackers are webinar based. And yes, we put a lot of attention in writing good show up sequences between when they sign up and when the webinar actually is. So we do coach that conversion and that conversion being showing up to the live webinar, which is a conversion event. So we do put a lot of effort and energy into that. But even if we did everything right, even if we put retargeting ads and reminder ads and all these things, we're still going to have at least 50% of people not show up. You know, 50% show up rate would be insanely high and just about every industry. In fact, at the end of the webinar, we're going to be left with three very distinct buckets. We're going to be left with those who registered and stayed till the end, right till the end. They saw everything, but just didn't buy. We're going to be left with people who didn't show up at all, aka ghosted and people who showed up but dropped off early and didn't get the pitch. And this is the most general bucket because they could have dropped off at any point. We don't necessarily know how much they saw. And even if we did a lot of webinar software will tell you when people came and went, you obviously can't write for, you know, hundreds of different buckets of people who stayed still five minutes and six minutes and seven minutes. So we do need to lump everyone who showed up, but then stay till the end of the same bucket. And we'll show you how to do that. So each prospect, each one of these segments has been coached to a certain degree towards the final conversion. You know, the prospect who stayed till the end has already been exposed to all your sales arguments, all the reasons for buying all the reasons to buy now, all the emotional and logical reasons, and therefore requires a much, much different follow up conversation than let's say the prospect who didn't show up at all, or the prospect who came, but left early and maybe didn't see the full offer. So since your webinar registrants, since your webinar registrants have displayed a level of intended commitment, because they did sign up, that is intent, that is commitment, it is totally worth investing the time and energy to market to these three segments differently. You don't want to waste the opportunity of just lumping them all in the exact same bucket. So the three part set of templates here is really used to engage each one of them in the most appropriate immediate post webinar conversation that hits on the points most likely to get to convert them into a buyer. Now they will all eventually converge into the same sales and closing sequence, but immediately after the webinar ends, this is where you really want to capitalize on the appropriate context of how much each segment has been coached. All right, so first segment, this one is for those who stayed till the end, they're going to get what we call the fab five. So what do these people need? They've already seen everything. They've seen all the logical information. They've had all the emotional appeals. They've seen your full pitch. They know what they're getting. They don't need all of that again in the immediate follow up email, but they need his urgency and nudge or reason to act now. They need courage and confidence to make that decision, not more logical information. They need to know that you are on their side and you could walk them through that decision and they need to feel exclusively empowered to make that decision. And a lot of that has to do with what bonus they could get if they sign up now for having stayed till the end. That is something that exclusively empowers them to take action, whereas those who didn't watch till the end don't have that luxury. So that's what they need more than anything. They don't need a repetition of the things that you already show them on the webinar, especially if they just stayed till the end and they're getting this email right now. So action items for this segment just offer a super high value stayed till the end bonus for max 24 hours or less. So this has been very effective at copy hackers and for a lot of clients I've written for anything more than 24 hours and you really lose that urgency factor. 48 hours kind of breeds procrastination, especially if it's around just a bonus that expires. And then send the fab five email template right after the webinar ends, not an hour later, not four hours later, not 10 hours later, right after the webinar ends. So this is the template. We're going to start off with what I call the what this says about you opening. So whenever you know an action they took in this case staying till the end, you get to unpack the meaning of that. What does that say about them? What does it say about who they are and or what they want or don't want, aka their motivations? What does it say about getting your help to create that outcome? Obviously, they've seen the product, they've seen what you could do. And what does it say about getting access to the for your eyes only bonus? So this is the exclusively empowerment because they stayed till the end. It means that they get access to that. So start with that opening reflecting back the action they took, what it means, then sell the value and benefits of the bonus, not the whole program that they've already sat through 60 minutes or 90 minutes of, but the exact thing that they are exclusively empowered to get access to. Then to call to action is framed around once again, the bonus and a confident close around why you want them to get the bonus and a final call to action, pushing the urgency and deadline, not of the offer as a whole, but of that 24 hour bonus. So that is the fab five. We're going to take a look at an example of that below this video, but for now we're going to wrap up the two other templates. So the next segment we're going to have after a webinar are the ghosts. These are people who registered for your webinar. So they showed that initial intent, but then they just didn't show up. So this is the no ghost shall pass. And what we mean by no ghost shall pass is that we're not going to send them directly to a sales page right away. Instead, what we're going to do is give them what they need. We need to reflect back what it means based off of their previous action. So first we got to show empathy around the no show, not guilting or shaming them or painting on some heavy FOMO. They did miss out, but that's not what they need. They don't need to have that painted on too thick. They need empathy around that. They need to feel like they didn't miss the boat. So a lot of people here, if they missed a webinar and they intended to be there, but they just couldn't swing it because life got in the way and that's usually what happens, then a lot of them will automatically disqualify themselves from the offer. They'll feel like they've already kind of missed the boat, the ship has sailed and they will disqualify themselves from becoming buyers and they don't have to do that. It's actually your job as the leader to get them back on track. And that's the next thing you're going to provide clarity on exactly how to get back on track and then reinforcement of that initial intent or motivation that have them signing up for that webinar. Yes, they are ghosts, but the initial action they took to sign up for the webinar is stronger than what it means that they missed it. If they've got in the way, maybe they couldn't get off or maybe their kids were sick, who knows what it was, but we're going to reinforce the initial motivation they had for signing up and holding them consistent to that. So clear action items, you want to create a webinar replay page and you want to offer a follow-up on-core presentation two or three days later. Ideally do both and you're going to see why we do both when we break down the example. But what we're doing here is essentially creating a choice. They can watch the webinar replay right away, which is more convenient, or they could schedule something a few days later. And we're actually creating a bit of an anchor, a convenience anchor between the two of them and a binary choice that both lead to them consuming the webinar. Most people will have a binary choice of either watch the replay or do nothing or come to the on-core or do nothing, but if we create the binary choice between a replay and an on-core, then both options are in service of them consuming the webinar and the information they need to receive. Cool. So here is the template as a whole. It follows the typical PASO formula, except this time we treat the pain as having missed the webinar and we agitate it by listing the reasons and cost of missing it. So pain, directly call it the fact that they missed it. Agitate diagnosis and just agitate the reasons why they may have missed it. But the important part here is to deflect the blame to external causes. Don't guilt them for having missed it. Don't say you promised to show up and you didn't. Show that you're on their side. This is where you can use gifts or emojis and just really write with empathy. Or be accusatory or blaming or shaming here to show that life probably got in the way. This is what may have happened. I'm on your side. Let's get you back on track. And then the solution is where you give the clear path to resolving that, which is either the webinar or the next live presentation. Once again, binary choice between two favorable outcomes rather than the choice being between watch the replay or do nothing. Cool. All right. So let's look at an example of that one also below. But for now, final one is those who left early, aka the traffic light. So we don't know what time, how long they stayed. Or even if we do know how long every person stayed, we can't write for dozens or hundreds of segments. We need to lump the people who showed up, but didn't stay till the end all in one email. And this is where we use the traffic light system. So of course, this is the most general non-specific of the three segments. It's essentially anyone who showed up, but didn't stay. You're not sure how much. And therefore they have varying degrees of intent. They've all consumed different levels of information. So we'll use the traffic light system as an analogy for their readiness to buy and offer three separate call to actions to match those levels of intent. So what do they need? They need intent appropriate call to actions depending on where they are in the buying process and what they need to go to the next step, which are the crystal clear options and guidance either to the sales page, to a replay page or an encore or off the list altogether. Those are really the three options we're going to be giving them. And then the ability to self segment and reorient and reorient and reengage in your launch based off of number one and number two. So action items, you're going to want to have a replay page again. You're going to want to have a long form sales page or at the very least a checkout page to send those who are ready to buy to. And you're also going to want to create an opt out lining page for those who just don't want anything to do with your product anymore. But we're going to make them decide on that. That's really the key to this template. They need to make a decision. They need to orient around where they're at. So the template goes as follows. Step one, reflect back the fact that they were on the webinar, which just reinforces the commitment. Hey, you showed up. Glad we had you. It's just reminding them of the fact that they've already invested time, energy and attention into this, into this webinar. Step number two, immediately pivot to offering clear direction and next steps. And so you don't know how much they've been coached towards that conversion. You immediately want to pivot to the traffic light segmentation system. So green light are for people who nearly stay till the end are and are almost there. We're going to have a call to action to the sales page. Yellow light is for people who either didn't watch much or are still very low intent. We're going to send them to a replay and red light are for people who have no interest regardless of how much they watched. And we're going to offer them a link to unsubscribe. And then step four is really we're just going to borderline insist that they take one of those actions to reengage, recommit or opt out of the launch altogether. So that's what it looks like. We're going to see all these templates in action in the video below. So meet me there and enjoy this gift, which I think it's from Casper the ghost.
